title: Finding a Percent of a Number

epigraph.text:
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, 
pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into 
your lap.

content.text:
One of the most practical percent skills is finding a percent of a 
number. You use this when calculating tips, discounts, taxes, and 
many other real-world quantities.
Finding a Percent of a Number

The word "of" in mathematics usually means multiply. To find a 
percent of a number, convert the percent to a decimal and multiply.

content.title: Finding a Percent of a Number
content.content:
To find $p\%$ of a number $n$:

1. Convert the percent to a decimal: $p\% = \frac{p}{100}$

2. Multiply: $\frac{p}{100} \times n$

content.title: Finding a percent of a number
Calculating a tip
content.problem: A restaurant bill is \$45. Calculate a 20\% tip.

content.text: For quick calculations, use these mental math strategies:
**Finding 10\%:** Move the decimal point one place left.
**Finding 1\%:** Move the decimal point two places left.

content.title: Quick tip calculation
content.problem: A meal costs \$72. Calculate a 20\% tip mentally.

content.text:
A discount reduces the price by a certain percent. The sale price 
equals the original price minus the discount.

content.title: Calculating Sale Price
content.content:
To find the sale price after a discount:

1. Calculate the discount: (original price) $\times$ (discount \%)

2. Subtract from original: (original price) $-$ (discount)

**Shortcut:** Multiply by (100\% $-$ discount \%)

content.title: Finding a sale price
content.problem:
A jacket originally priced at \$80 is on sale for 25\% off. What 
is the sale price?

content.text:
Sales tax increases the price by a certain percent. The total price 
equals the original price plus the tax.

content.title: Calculating sales tax
content.problem: A video game costs \$60. If sales tax is 8\%, what is the total cost?

content.text:
When both a discount and sales tax apply, usually the discount is 
calculated first, then tax is applied to the sale price.

content.title: Discount then tax
content.problem:
A \$150 item is on sale for 20\% off. Sales tax is 6\%. What is 
the final price?
content.solution:
**Step 1:** Calculate the sale price.
\[
 \text{Sale price} = \$150 \times 0.80 = \$120
\]

**Step 2:** Calculate tax on the sale price.
\[
 \text{Tax} = \$120 \times 0.06 = \$7.20
\]

**Step 3:** Add tax to sale price.
\[
 \text{Final price} = \$120 + \$7.20 = \$127.20
\]

content.content:
You cannot simply subtract the discount percent and add the tax 
percent! A 20\% discount followed by 6\% tax is not the same as 
a 14\% discount. The order and base amount matter.

content.title: Honest Dealings
content.content:
Proverbs repeatedly emphasizes honest weights and measures 
(11:1, 16:11, 20:10, 20:23). Understanding percent calculations 
protects us from being cheated and enables us to deal honestly 
with others. A store that advertises 30\% off but calculates 
discounts incorrectly is engaging in dishonest practice.

marginNotes.text:
Be sure to set aside a tenth of 
all that your fields produce each year.

She sees that her trading is 
profitable, and her lamp does not go out at night.

marginNotes.author: Blaise Pascal
marginNotes.text:
The heart has its reasons which reason 
knows nothing of.

marginNotes.author: Leonard Euler
marginNotes.text:
Mathematicians have tried in vain to this 
day to discover some order in the sequence of prime numbers, and we 
have reason to believe that it is a mystery into which the human 
mind will never penetrate.
